scores.correctness_notes: Correctly covers the change/task model structure, states, and all major ways to check completion including wait_change(), polling via get_change(), get_changes() filtering, and abort. Misses CLI approach (pebble changes/tasks commands), Notices (change-update), and the state file location, but the core questions are answered well with extra correct detail on state transitions.
scores.hallucination_notes: container.get_pebble_client() appears to be a fabricated method — in ops, the Container object is the interface and there is no public get_pebble_client() method exposed. Also ops.pebble.ChangeState.IN_PROGRESS used as a select param is slightly off from the string-based select approach in the gold standard, though the class itself exists. The state table and wait_change() behavior descriptions appear accurate.
